verses.2: “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge, And fill himself with the east wind?
verses.3: “Should he argue with useless talk, Or with words which do not benefit?
verses.4: “Indeed, you do away with reverence, And hinder meditation before God.
verses.5: “For your wrongdoing teaches your mouth, And you choose the language of the cunning.
verses.6: “Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; And your own lips testify against you.
verses.7: “Were you the first person to be born, Or were you brought forth before the hills?
verses.8: “Do you hear the secret discussion of God, And limit wisdom to yourself?
verses.9: “What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that we do not?
verses.10: “Both the gray-haired and the aged are among us, Older than your father.
verses.11: “Are the consolations of God too little for you, Or the word spoken gently to you?
verses.12: “Why does your heart take you away? And why do your eyes wink,
verses.13: That you can turn your spirit against God And produce such words from your mouth?
verses.14: “What is man, that he would be pure, Or he who is born of a woman, that he would be righteous?
verses.15: “Behold, He has no trust in His holy ones, And the heavens are not pure in His sight;
verses.16: How much less one who is detestable and corrupt: A person who drinks malice like water!
verses.17: “I will tell you, listen to me; And what I have seen I will also declare;
verses.18: What wise people have told, And have not concealed from their fathers,
verses.19: To whom alone the land was given, And no stranger passed among them.
verses.20: “The wicked person writhes in pain all his days, And the years reserved for the ruthless are numbered.
verses.21: “Sounds of terror are in his ears; While he is at peace the destroyer comes upon him.
verses.22: “He does not believe that he will return from darkness, And he is destined for the sword.
verses.23: “He wanders about for food, saying, ‘Where is it? ’ He knows that a day of darkness is at hand.
verses.24: “Distress and anguish terrify him, They overpower him like a king ready for the attack,
verses.25: Because he has reached out with his hand against God, And is arrogant toward the Almighty.
verses.26: “He rushes headlong at Him With his massive shield.
verses.27: “For he has covered his face with his fat, And put fat on his waist.
verses.28: “He has lived in desolate cities, In houses no one would inhabit, Which are destined to become ruins.
verses.29: “He will not become rich, nor will his wealth endure; And his property will not stretch out on the earth.
verses.30: “He will not escape from darkness; The flame will dry up his shoot, And he will go away by the breath of His mouth.
verses.31: “Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself; For his reward will be emptiness.
verses.32: “It will be accomplished before his time, And his palm branch will not be green.
verses.33: “He will drop off his unripe grape like the vine, And will cast off his flower like the olive tree.
verses.34: “For the company of the godless is barren, And fire consumes the tents of the corrupt.
verses.35: “They conceive harm and give birth to wrongdoing, And their mind prepares deception.”
